todaysEvents.summary: Brent falls to $96.50 Asia session before recovering to $103 US session as de-escalation optimism fades; Trump pause Day 2 holds with no new strikes; Mar 28 deadline 4 days away (Goodreturns.in, CNBC). (Mar 24)

Iran FM privately confirms to CBS News that Tehran received US message through mediators and is reviewing 15 points; Iran still publicly denies direct talks (Al Jazeera, CBS News). First confirmed back-channel. (Mar 24)
IMO reports ~2,000 vessels trapped in Persian Gulf; US CENTCOM states 9,000+ Iranian targets struck and 140+ Iranian naval vessels destroyed since conflict began Feb 28 (CNN, NBC News). (Mar 24)

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran's FM confirmed on Mar 24 (CBS News) that Tehran received US mediator message and is reviewing 15 points — first confirmed back-channel. If Trump's pause (expires Mar 28) catalyses formal engagement, partial Hormuz reopening within 2 weeks is possible. Brent retreats toward $85-95/bbl on ceasefire signal; JKM pullback to $18-20 as Qatar Ras Laffan damage is assessed. P&I cover restoration begins within 30 days. ASEAN oil-importer FX stabilises. Key risk: Iran publicly denies talks and IRGC attacked Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Mar 23.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Trump's pause expires Mar 28 with only cosmetic Iranian concessions; US refrains from power plant strikes but maintains current military posture. IRGC continues low-intensity attacks on Saudi and US targets. Hormuz selective-passage framework persists; Ras Laffan restart delayed 8-12 weeks. Brent settles $95-115/bbl; JKM at $20-24/MMBtu with no Qatar restart path. ASEAN oil-importer FX (IDR, PHP) under sustained pressure; iTraxx Asia IG at 170-185bp. Arab League ceasefire framework remains rhetorical.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Trump's pause expires with no progress; US strikes Iranian power plants, triggering IRGC full escalation. Iran activates Strait of Malacca closure threat; full commercial Hormuz and Red Sea blockade; Ras Laffan permanently offline for the quarter. Oil hits $160-190/bbl; LNG spot markets seize. ASEAN recession risk surges — Thailand and Philippines face double-digit fuel inflation and sovereign credit-rating action. Iran's public denial of talks and IRGC attacks on Bahrain and Saudi Arabia Mar 23 confirm Iran is not fully standing down despite back-channel activity.
